name: Leon Thomas
abstract: Amos Leon Thomas Jr. (October 4, 1937 – May 8, 1999), known professionally as Leon Thomas, was an American jazz and blues vocalist, born in East St.

concerts.label: Praise the Lord in Many Voices

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: Leon Thomas is a standout at the piano and also doubles on the electric violin for sweet work.

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Bunnett
about_quotes.evidence: I just feel like I’m influenced by so many things that I just hear, particular pieces like Pharoah Sanders and Leon Thomas, “The Creator Has a Master Plan.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Bunnett interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 25, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Lonnie Liston Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Leon Thomas always got together... Pharoah said man, you know why don’t we all just get together? And it worked.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lonnie Liston Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: you used to could see Betty Carter, Miles Davis, Jimmy Smith, Leon Thomas, Moms Mabley, "Pig Meat" Markham.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote a show called Sing Me a Jazz Song. I had Leon Thomas, Eddie, Annie, and me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
